title: Mildly garlicky Indian skirt steak Sambhar
description: A comforting Indian dish featuring skirt steak, a mix of spices, and a cheesy pull from within. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Season the skirt steak with garam masala, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the skirt steak for 2-3 minutes per side, or until browned.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 12-15 minutes, or until the skirt steak reaches 130°F (54°C) for medium-rare.
While the skirt steak is cooking, sauté the onion and garlic in a separate skillet until softened.
Add the chana masala to the skillet and stir to combine.
Remove the skirt steak from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes.
Top the skirt steak with the onion and garlic mixture, followed by the cheddar cheese.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before slicing against the grain.
Slice the skirt steak into thin strips and serve with the cheesy onion and garlic mixture.
